description: FDS motion system — canonical specs for every animation in the design system. Machine-readable companion to foundations.md §Motion. Use this file for all timing, easing, stagger, and state-transition decisions.

extraFast.use: Button press feedback ONLY — the fastest perceptible response

instant.use: State changes (hover, focus, active), icon swaps, colour transitions

quick.use: Content transitions, accordion expand/collapse, dropdown open, tab indicator

gentle.use: Modal entrance/exit, drawer slide, sidesheet, overlay fade

entry.use: Elements entering the viewport — decelerates to rest

exit.use: Elements leaving the viewport — accelerates away

default.use: In-place state transitions — enters fast, exits gently

linear.use: Looping animations only (skeleton shimmer, loading spinners)

press.note: Use 0.97 for button press — 0.8 is too aggressive for UI

stagger.rule: Add 60ms delay per successive item in a list, grid, or group entrance
stagger.maxDelay: 480ms — never stagger beyond 8 items; remaining items share the final delay
stagger.useWhen: List items entering screen, card grids loading, nav link reveals
stagger.avoidWhen: Content already on screen, user-triggered single actions
stagger.cssPattern: .f-list__item { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(8px); animation: f-list-enter var(--f-brand-motion-duration-quick) var(--f-brand-motion-easing-entry) forwards; } .f-list__item:nth-child(1) { animation-delay: 0ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(2) { animation-delay: 60ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(3) { animation-delay: 120ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(4) { animation-delay: 180ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(5) { animation-delay: 240ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(6) { animation-delay: 300ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(7) { animation-delay: 360ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(8) { animation-delay: 420ms; } .f-list__item:nth-child(n+9) { animation-delay: 420ms; } @keyframes f-list-enter { to { opacity: 1; transform: none; } }

loading-to-success.note: Success icon scales in after spinner fades

loading-to-error.note: Error is immediate — never animate errors in slowly

gpuRules.heightTrick: Use grid-template-rows: 0fr → 1fr for smooth height animation without animating height directly
gpuRules.willChange: Add will-change: transform only immediately before animation starts; remove on animationend/transitionend
gpuRules.compositeOnly: Only properties that are GPU-composited (transform, opacity) guarantee 60fps on low-end devices

interruptionRules.principle: All animations must be interruptible by user action
interruptionRules.cssTransitions: CSS transitions are inherently interruptible — prefer over CSS animations for reversible states
interruptionRules.cssAnimations: Use CSS @keyframes only for looping or one-shot effects (skeleton, success pulse)
exceptions: Loading spinners may complete their current cycle
Page transitions should not interrupt (disable pointer events during)
interruptionRules.pattern: Never use animation-fill-mode: forwards on interactive state animations — use CSS transitions instead

reducedMotion.css: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }
reducedMotion.rule: Every @keyframes animation must have a prefers-reduced-motion equivalent. No exceptions.

luxuryPrinciples.restraint: One micro-interaction per component — not a showcase of every effect
luxuryPrinciples.purposeful: Every animation must communicate state change or guide attention — never decorative
luxuryPrinciples.premium: Animations feel confident and unhurried — not bouncy, not snappy, not playful
